fixture_name: M14 External Agent Skill Capability Supply Chain And Skill Admission Gate Fixtures

supply_chain_positioning.external_artifact_role: NVIDIA skills are external agent capability artifacts.
supply_chain_positioning.aaos_role: AAOS is the capability admission, permission, evidence, replay, escalation, and decision-governance control plane.
supply_chain_positioning.vendor_governance: AAOS governance remains model-agnostic and vendor-agnostic.

skill_admission_contract.description: A synthetic, non-executing contract specimen for a read-only Jetson diagnostic capability; it contains no real NVIDIA skill code or device data.
intended_use: admission-policy evaluation using synthetic fixture data
prohibited_use: live device access during fixture evaluation
known_risks: permission declaration may drift from observed requirements
artifact identity may drift after review

fail_closed_rules.condition: actual required permission exceeds declared permission
signature verification evidence is required but missing
scan evidence is required but missing
high-risk use lacks benchmark or evaluation evidence
artifact digest no longer matches the reviewed artifact
source uses only a mutable branch or tag without immutable binding

default_posture: High-risk domain without evaluation evidence = blocked
required_boundary_statements: External skill capability is not governance permission.
Skill metadata is not verified behavior.
Skill installation is not execution authorization.
Artifact signature is not governance approval.
Signature verification is not risk acceptance.
Scan passed is not risk accepted.
Benchmark passed is not deployment approval.
Evaluation evidence is not final approval.
candidate_allowed is not execution approval.
needs_approval is not approval granted.
admission_ready_for_review is not final admission approval.
fail_closed_recommended is not fail_closed_executed.
rollback_recommended is not rollback_executed.
human_review_required is not completed review.
evidence_complete is not Decision Proof sealing.
replay_ready is not Decision Proof sealing.
Registry classification is not final governance judgment.
NVIDIA skills remain external capability artifacts.
AAOS remains vendor-independent.
Decision Proof sealing remains AAOS-owned.
AAOS remains the decision sovereignty layer.

fixture_cases.description: The complete synthetic, non-executing baseline is ready to be routed to admission review only.
An unknown source cannot establish provenance or immutable review identity.
A source repository without a declared source owner is not reviewable.
A repository name alone does not bind the reviewed artifact.
A source commit without an artifact digest is incomplete artifact identity.
A mutable tag is not an immutable review binding even when other metadata is present.
The reviewed version is required in addition to commit and digest.
License metadata is required before candidate admission review.
Admission requires an accountable skill owner distinct from source ownership.
A policy-required signature must be present, while never becoming governance approval.
Signature metadata without the required verification evidence is insufficient.
A required scan evidence reference must be present and remains non-authoritative for risk acceptance.
Shell access must be explicitly declared even when the declaration is none.
Network access must be explicitly declared even when network is denied.
File access and its scope must be explicitly declared.
MCP access must be explicitly declared even when no MCP server is requested.
Secret access must be explicitly declared even when no secret may be accessed.
An observed shell requirement outside the declared no-shell envelope is a blocked escalation.
The candidate is blocked when required isolation cannot be provided.
Outputs without a schema and allowed-value contract cannot be evaluated safely.
Missing trace requirements prevent accountability and replay linkage.
The admission evaluator requires replay evidence production without executing the skill.
A skill past its reassessment interval is routed for reassessment rather than approved.
Evidence expired before the fixed evaluation time cannot support admission review.
An observed artifact digest that differs from the reviewed digest invalidates admission evidence.
A synthetic medical-domain candidate cannot proceed without required benchmark and evaluation evidence.
A synthetic physical-action candidate requires both sandboxing and human-review routing.
Synthetic signature evidence cannot cure an excessive permission envelope or reviewed-permission drift.
Synthetic scan evidence cannot cure undeclared outbound network requirements.
The evaluator must reject any conversion of admission readiness into execution approval.
Signature verification evidence is an input, never governance approval.
A scan result cannot accept risk on behalf of AAOS governance.
Benchmark evidence cannot authorize deployment.
The data-only evaluator must reject and never perform skill execution.
Risk acceptance remains outside evaluator authority.
Decision Proof sealing remains AAOS-owned and cannot be performed by this evaluator.
The evaluator cannot transfer AAOS governance authority.
The M14 tracker remains open and the evaluator cannot close it.
M14 remains active work and cannot be declared complete by this fixture or evaluator.
v0.13.0 is a future target and cannot be declared released by this fixture or evaluator.
